The East-West Schism and early eastern Christianity are relevant to 822.F.3 on religion of the pre-Christian Slavic peoples and their eventual Christianization.

Second Temple Period manuscripts illuminate early Jewish sectarianism and the Essene community within the context of Syrian and Palestinian religions, and the religious milieu from which early Christianity and mystery religions emerged in the Hellenistic world.

Alexander's conquests from Greece to Central Asia catalyzed the Hellenistic cultural and religious synthesis, directly shaping the religious landscape of the Hellenistic world including the spread of Greek religion and syncretistic cults.

Covers magic, alchemy, astrology, prophecy, and sacrifice rooted in ancient and pre-Christian European traditions, connecting ancient quasi-scientific and magical cults of the Hellenistic world to pre-Christian Slavic religious practices.

Examines magic as idea and practice across millennia including ancient ritual practices, the boundary between magic, science, and religion, and figures from Roger Bacon to Max Weber, directly relevant to quasi-scientific and magical cults in the Hellenistic world.

Ancient Greek and Roman natural philosophy from the 8th century BCE through the 6th century CE provides intellectual context for understanding Greek and Roman religious thought, including Neoplatonism, Stoicism, and Epicureanism as religious philosophies.

Covers cuneiform and Egyptian hieroglyphic writing systems central to understanding Mesopotamian religious texts and Egyptian religious literature, and addresses Mayan glyphs relevant to pre-Columbian Mayan religion.

Hebrew Bible situated within ancient Near Eastern context illuminates Syrian and Palestinian religions through its engagement with Canaanite deities, covenant theology, prophetic traditions, and archaeological evidence from Mesopotamia and Egypt.

Historical roots of superstition in ancient Greek, Roman, and Chinese cultures directly inform understanding of quasi-scientific and magical cults such as numerology and astrology in the Hellenistic world.
